title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure poses a significant threat to organizations relying on cloud services. By exploiting compromised CI/CD pipelines, attackers can deploy malicious code, potentially leading to data encryption, service disruptions, and financial losses.
recommended_actions: Immediately review and secure CI/CD pipelines by implementing multi-factor authentication (MFA) and rotating access tokens.
Conduct a thorough audit of cloud infrastructure to identify and patch any vulnerabilities or misconfigurations.
Implement robust backup strategies and ensure backups are stored offline or in secure, isolated environments.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself poses minimal direct risk; however, widespread adoption could lead to exploitation if the tool contains unpatched vulnerabilities or is used improperly. Malicious actors could also create fake versions to distribute malware.
recommended_actions: Evaluate the credibility and security of the tool before adoption.
Monitor for updates or patches to the tool and apply them promptly.
Educate teams on verifying the authenticity of open-source tools to avoid counterfeit versions.

title: A vulnerability has been found in liangliangyy DjangoBlog up to 2.1.0.0. The impacted element is...
description: A vulnerability has been found in liangliangyy DjangoBlog up to 2.1.0.0. The impacted element is an unknown function of the file djangoblog/settings.py of the component Setting Handler. Such manipulation of the argument USER/PASSWORD leads to hard-coded credentials. The attack may be launched remotely. The attack requires a high level of complexity. The exploitability is regarded as difficult. The exploit has been disclosed to the public and may be used. The vendor was contacted early about this

title: A vulnerability was detected in lm-sys fastchat up to 0.2.36. Impacted is the function add_text...
description: A vulnerability was detected in lm-sys fastchat up to 0.2.36. Impacted is the function add_text of the component Arena Side-by-Side View Handler. The manipulation results in incorrect control flow. The attack can be launched remotely. The exploit is now public and may be used. The root cause was fixed in commit 34eca62 for gradio_block_arena_named.py, but three other files were missed.

title: A vulnerability was found in rickxy Hospital Management System up to...
description: A vulnerability was found in rickxy Hospital Management System up to 88a4290d957dc5bdde8a56e5ad451ad14f7f90f4. Affected is an unknown function of the file /backend/admin/his_admin_account.php. The manipulation of the argument ad_dpic results in unrestricted upload. The attack can be executed remotely. The exploit has been made public and could be used. This product implements a rolling release for ongoing delivery, which means version information for affected or updated releases is unavailable.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows for unrestricted file uploads, which could lead to remote code execution, data breaches, or unauthorized access to sensitive patient data. This poses a significant threat to the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of the hospital management system.
recommended_actions: Immediately disable or restrict access to the vulnerable endpoint (/backend/admin/his_admin_account.php) until a patch is available.
Implement strict file upload validation and sanitization to prevent malicious files from being uploaded.
Monitor for any suspicious activity or unauthorized access attempts and apply updates or patches as soon as they are released.

title: Initialization of a resource with an insecure default vulnerability exists in SD-330AC and AMC...
description: Initialization of a resource with an insecure default vulnerability exists in SD-330AC and AMC Manager provided by silex technology, Inc. When the affected device is connected to the network with the initial (factory-default) configuration, the device can be configured with the null string password.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-32965.

title: A vulnerability has been found in Lagom WHMCS Template up to 2.4.2. This impacts an unknown...
description: A vulnerability has been found in Lagom WHMCS Template up to 2.4.2. This impacts an unknown function of the component Datatables. The manipulation leads to resource consumption. Remote exploitation of the attack is possible. The exploit has been disclosed to the public and may be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Lagom WHMCS Template up to version 2.4.2 allows for resource consumption attacks, potentially leading to denial-of-service (DoS) conditions. This could disrupt operations for businesses relying on WHMCS for billing and client management. The public disclosure of the exploit increases the likelihood of exploitation.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the Lagom WHMCS Template to a patched version if available, or apply any provided mitigations from the vendor.
Implement web application firewall (WAF) rules to monitor and block suspicious activity targeting the Datatables component.
Conduct a thorough review of server resource usage and enable rate-limiting to mitigate potential resource exhaustion attacks.

title: SD-330AC and AMC Manager provided by silex technology, Inc. contain an improper neutralization of...
description: SD-330AC and AMC Manager provided by silex technology, Inc. contain an improper neutralization of CRLF sequences ('CRLF Injection') vulnerability. Processing some crafted configuration data may lead to arbitrary entries injected to the system configuration.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The CRLF Injection vulnerability in SD-330AC and AMC Manager could allow attackers to inject arbitrary entries into the system configuration, potentially leading to unauthorized system changes, data manipulation, or exploitation of downstream systems.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest patches or firmware updates provided by silex technology, Inc. to address the vulnerability.
Restrict access to the affected systems and ensure only trusted users can modify configuration data.
Monitor system logs for any unusual or unauthorized configuration changes and implement input validation to prevent exploitation.

title: A flaw has been found in langflow-ai langflow up to 1.8.3. This affects an unknown function of...
description: A flaw has been found in langflow-ai langflow up to 1.8.3. This affects an unknown function of the file src/frontend/src/modals/IOModal/components/chatView/chatMessage/components/edit-message.tsx of the component Frontend React Component Rendering. Executing a manipulation can lead to cross site scripting. The attack may be launched remotely. The exploit has been published and may be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the langflow-ai langflow frontend React component allows for cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks. This could enable attackers to inject malicious scripts, potentially compromising user data, session tokens, or other sensitive information. The exploit is remotely executable and has been publicly disclosed, increasing the likelihood of exploitation.
recommended_actions: Immediately update or patch the langflow-ai langflow library to a version beyond 1.8.3 if available. If no patch is available, consider applying temporary mitigations such as input sanitization or disabling the vulnerable component.

Conduct a thorough code review of the affected file (src/frontend/src/modals/IOModal/components/chatView/chatMessage/components/edit-message.tsx) and other related components to identify and address potential vulnerabilities.
Monitor systems for unusual activity or signs of exploitation, and implement a web application firewall (WAF) to block potential XSS payloads.

title: A security flaw has been discovered in modelscope agentscope up to 1.0.18. This affects the...
description: A security flaw has been discovered in modelscope agentscope up to 1.0.18. This affects the function _get_bytes_from_web_url of the file src/agentscope/_utils/_common.py of the component Internal Service. Performing a manipulation results in server-side request forgery. It is possible to initiate the attack remotely. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in modelscope agentscope up to version 1.0.18 allows for server-side request forgery (SSRF), which could enable attackers to access internal systems, exfiltrate sensitive data, or perform unauthorized actions on behalf of the server. The public availability of the exploit increases the likelihood of widespread attacks.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade modelscope agentscope to a version beyond 1.0.18, if available. If no patch is available, consider temporarily disabling or restricting access to the vulnerable component.
Implement strict input validation and sanitization for any user-supplied URLs to mitigate SSRF risks.
Monitor network traffic and server logs for unusual or unauthorized requests, and deploy web application firewalls (WAFs) to block malicious payloads targeting this vulnerability.

Implement input validation and sanitization for image_url/audio_file_url parameters to prevent malicious manipulation.
Monitor server logs for unusual or unauthorized requests targeting the affected endpoints and apply network-level restrictions where feasible.

title: SD-330AC and AMC Manager provided by silex technology, Inc. contain an issue with a sensitive...
description: SD-330AC and AMC Manager provided by silex technology, Inc. contain an issue with a sensitive information in resource not removed before reuse.  An attacker may login to the device without knowing the password by sending a crafted packet.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows an attacker to bypass authentication by sending a crafted packet, potentially leading to unauthorized access to the device and sensitive data exposure. This could also enable further exploitation of connected systems.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply any available patches or firmware updates provided by silex technology, Inc. for the affected devices.
Implement network segmentation to isolate vulnerable devices and restrict access to trusted sources only.
Monitor network traffic for suspicious activities, such as unexpected crafted packets targeting the affected devices.
